transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-07-20]
[00:00] You: Let's discuss the mobile app strategy.
[00:25] Speaker 1: We considered building a native iOS app earlier in the year, but we decided not to pursue it — the web app covers our primary use cases.
[00:55] Speaker 2: Agreed. The decision was to invest in the PWA instead. We also looked at React Native at one point but dropped it.
[01:25] Speaker 1: Right. The PWA initiative is what we're going with. I'll finalize the PWA feature spec by July 25th.
[01:55] You: What about push notifications for mobile users?
[02:10] Speaker 2: We decided NOT to implement push notifications in this cycle — the privacy policy updates would be too complex. That's deferred to next year.
[02:40] Speaker 3: The one thing we did decide to build is the mobile-optimized checkout flow. I'll own that. I'll have the implementation plan ready by July 23rd.
[03:10] You: Great. Let's stay focused on the PWA and checkout — nothing else this cycle.

adversarial_injections.description: Native iOS app was explicitly decided against — 'we decided not to pursue it' is a rejected idea, not a current commitment
React Native was evaluated and dropped — past tense dropped idea, not a new commitment
Push notifications were explicitly decided NOT to implement this cycle — an explicit rejection should not be extracted as an action item or decision to implement

action_items.source_quote: I'll finalize the PWA feature spec by July 25th.

action_items.description: Create implementation plan for mobile-optimized checkout flow
action_items.source_quote: I'll have the implementation plan ready by July 23rd.

decisions: Mobile strategy: invest in PWA instead of native iOS or React Native
Push notifications deferred to next year due to privacy policy complexity
